corrosim — multiscale corrosion-inhibitor report

Substrate Fe(110)  |  Medium 1 M HCl  |  DFT level B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)  |  Generated 2026-07-01 11:20

Flavonoids modelled here (kaempferol, quercetin, isorhamnetin) are documented major constituents of the extract, simulated as representatives — not a verified profile of a specific sample. Confirm composition by LC-MS/GC-MS before publication.

kaempferol4.1462.0730.4820.169-16.553.35-1.368

Composite score z-scores a smaller gap, lower hardness and higher softness (higher = stronger predicted reactivity). Eads (Stage 2 Monte Carlo) and the Fe–O distance (Stage 3 MD) are shown alongside for the adsorption picture.

Geometry refinement (FF vs DFT-optimised)

Relaxing each structure at B3LYP/6-31G(d) before the production single point lowers the gap (~0.4–0.5 eV) and hardness and raises ΔN, but leaves both the gap and ΔN rankings unchanged — the lead assignments are geometry-robust.

Optimised-geometry descriptors (DFT-relaxed)

The same reactivity descriptors on DFT-optimised geometries (B3LYP/6-31G(d) relaxation, then the production single point) instead of the force-field geometries used for the headline table. The gap/softness composite ranking is unchanged (gap quercetin < isorhamnetin < kaempferol; ΔN isorhamnetin > quercetin > kaempferol) — the lead is geometry-robust.

Optimised protonated cations (in-acid)

The DFT-optimised +1 cations — the more accurate geometric basis for the speciation / pKaH work (ADR 0004/0005) than the force-field cations.

isorhamnetin+H+C16H13O7+1B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)-6.269-3.2613.0081.5040.6654.7657.5470.018-0.376-7.044

Species in the acidic medium (protonated cation)

In 1 M HCl the basic carbonyl / hydroxyl oxygens take up a proton, so the inhibitor is present largely as its +1 cation. The ranking above uses the neutral form (the conventional descriptor basis); the protonated-cation descriptors are tabulated here for comparison.

nameformulachargelevelhomo_evlumo_evgap_evhardness_evsoftness_inv_evelectronegativity_evelectrophilicity_evdelta_nback_donation_evtnc
kaempferol+H+C15H11O6+1B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)-6.881-3.2773.6041.8020.5555.0797.157-0.072-0.451-4.014
quercetin+H+C15H11O7+1B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)-6.668-3.3243.3441.6720.5984.9967.465-0.053-0.418-4.438
isorhamnetin+H+C16H13O7+1B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)-6.427-3.2953.1321.5660.6394.8617.546-0.013-0.391-4.993

Protonation lowers the gap and raises softness, and ΔN turns negative — the electron-poor cation no longer donates to the metal, so the ΔN > 0 donation heuristic does not apply to this form (cation adsorption is electrostatic / back-donation driven). The most reactive species by the gap/softness composite is therefore form-dependent, which is why the neutral form is kept as the headline ranking (ADR 0003).

Speciation in 1 M HCl (pH ≈ -0.0)

The most basic site of these flavonoids is the 4-oxo carbonyl, a very weak base (estimated conjugate-acid pKaH ≈ -1.5; ADR 0004). By Henderson–Hasselbalch the inhibitor is 97% neutral / 3% protonated at this pH — the neutral form dominates, which is why the headline ranking uses the neutral form.

Population-weighted (pH-weighted) descriptors — blended lead: quercetin:

nameformulachargelevelhomo_evlumo_evgap_evhardness_evsoftness_inv_evelectronegativity_evelectrophilicity_evdelta_nback_donation_evtnc
kaempferolC15H10O60B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)-6.214-2.0854.1292.0640.4854.1494.1880.162-0.516-4.41
quercetinC15H10O70B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)-6.151-2.0914.062.030.4934.1214.2070.171-0.507-4.705
isorhamnetinC16H12O70B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)-6.022-1.9524.0692.0350.4923.9873.9380.203-0.509-5.509

Sensitivity. The gap/softness composite lead changes from quercetin to isorhamnetin at only ~7% protonation (pKaH ≈ -1.1); the pKaH±1 band (0%–24% protonated) straddles that crossover — so the lead is sensitive to the protonation pKa, the key uncertainty here.

Computed pKaH (DFT deprotonation cycle)

moleculecomputed pKaH% protonated @ this pH
kaempferol-11.20.00%
quercetin-12.10.00%
isorhamnetin-3.30.05%

From a B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) + ddCOSMO aqueous deprotonation cycle (electronic-only; `results/pka.json`, ADR 0005). All values sit far below the crossover — the most basic flavonoid is only 0.05% protonated — so every species is essentially fully neutral here. This resolves the sensitivity above: the neutral form is the physically dominant species, not just the conventional choice, so the headline lead is robust. The omitted O–H zero-point energy would push pKaH lower still (more neutral), reinforcing this.

Stage 1b  Local reactivity (Fukui)

Condensed Fukui / dual-descriptor maps locate the donor and acceptor atoms. The strongest electron-donating oxygens (highest f⁻) are the metal-coordinating sites:

  • kaempferol: O3 (f⁻=0.116), O12 (f⁻=0.055), O19 (f⁻=0.053)
  • quercetin: O3 (f⁻=0.090), O9 (f⁻=0.088), O11 (f⁻=0.040)
  • isorhamnetin: O9 (f⁻=0.094), O3 (f⁻=0.093), O11 (f⁻=0.043)

Electrostatic-potential (ESP) map

The electron-density isosurface coloured by the molecular electrostatic potential. Red (negative) regions over the catechol and carbonyl oxygens are the electron-rich, metal-coordinating sites — corroborating the Fukui donor analysis above.

Stage 2  Monte Carlo adsorption

Metropolis/annealing pose search on the metal surface. Flavonoids settle flat/parallel; Eads ≈ −16 kJ/mol indicates physisorption.

Stage 3  Brownian MD — Fe–O RDF

Rigid-body Brownian dynamics from the best MC pose; the first Fe–O radial-distribution peak (~3.5 Å) sets the adsorption distance.

Method & caveats

Descriptors from frontier-orbital energies (Koopmans). DFT level: B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water). ΔN uses the metal work function with η(metal)=0. Monte Carlo / Brownian MD use a classical van-der-Waals adsorption model — a screening surrogate, not a substitute for periodic DFT or electrochemical validation. The composite ranking is a heuristic.
